subject: acute flaccid paralysis (6th year)
description: Acute flaccid paralysis: sudden onset weakness with reduced tone and reflexes, causes include Guillain-Barré syndrome, poliomyelitis, transverse myelitis

subject: Acute gastroenteritis
description: Acute gastroenteritis: diarrhea, vomiting, dehydration, viral gastroenteritis, ORS, rotavirus

subject: ADHD
description: ADHD: attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity, behavioral disorder

subject: AKD & CKD
description: AKD & CKD: acute kidney disease, chronic kidney disease, renal failure, elevated creatinine, pediatric nephrology

subject: Anemia
description: Anemia: microcytic anemia, iron deficiency, hemolytic anemia, megaloblastic anemia, pallor, hemoglobin

subject: approach to inborn errors of metabolism (6th year)
description: Inborn errors of metabolism: inherited metabolic disorders presenting with vomiting, developmental delay, metabolic acidosis, hypoglycemia; requires systematic diagnostic approach

subject: Asthma
description: Asthma: wheeze, reversible airway obstruction, bronchodilator, inhaled steroids, pediatric asthma

subject: asthma/allergic rhinitis sinusitis (6th year)
description: spectrum of allergic airway diseases involving wheeze, nasal congestion, sneezing, and sinus inflammation

subject: Autistic spectrum disorder
description: Autistic spectrum disorder: ASD, social communication deficit, repetitive behavior, autism screening, neurodevelopmental disorder

subject: Bacterial Neonatal Sepsis
description: Bacterial Neonatal Sepsis: early onset sepsis, late onset sepsis, GBS, neonatal infection, antibiotics, septic workup

subject: Bleeding disorders
description: Bleeding disorders: hemophilia, von Willebrand disease, platelet disorders, coagulopathy, purpura

subject: Bronchiolitis
description: Bronchiolitis: RSV, wheezing infant, viral bronchiolitis, respiratory distress, lower respiratory infection

subject: Cerebral palsy
description: Cerebral palsy: spastic CP, motor disability, perinatal insult, developmental delay, cerebral palsy classification

subject: child abuse (6th year)
description: Child abuse: physical, emotional, or sexual harm to a child, presenting with unexplained injuries, developmental issues, or behavioral changes

subject: Chronic diarrhea & malabsorption
description: Chronic diarrhea & malabsorption: celiac disease, chronic diarrhea, steatorrhea, failure to thrive, malabsorption syndrome

subject: Common chromosomal anomalies
description: Common chromosomal anomalies: Down syndrome, Turner syndrome, Klinefelter syndrome, trisomy 18, trisomy 13

subject: Common problems in neonates
description: Common problems in neonates: hypothermia, hypoglycemia, jaundice, feeding difficulty, sepsis, respiratory distress

subject: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia
description: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia: CAH, 21-hydroxylase deficiency, salt wasting, ambiguous genitalia, adrenal crisis

subject: congenital immunodeficiency (6th year)
description: Congenital immunodeficiency: primary immune disorders leading to recurrent infections, involving B-cell, T-cell, or combined defects (e.g., SCID)

subject: constipation (6th year)
description: Constipation: infrequent or difficult bowel movements in children, often functional, associated with stool withholding, abdominal pain, and hard stools

subject: cyanotic & acyanotic heart disease
description: Cyanotic & acyanotic heart disease: congenital heart disease, VSD, ASD, PDA, TOF, murmur, shunt lesions

subject: Cyanotic heart disease
description: Cyanotic heart disease: TOF, transposition of great arteries, cyanosis, right-to-left shunt, clubbing

subject: Cystic Fibrosis
description: Cystic Fibrosis: CF, recurrent chest infection, pancreatic insufficiency, salty sweat, bronchiectasis

subject: Developmental assessment
description: Developmental assessment: developmental milestones, gross motor, fine motor, speech, social development, developmental delay

subject: DKA (6th year)
description: Diabetic ketoacidosis: acute complication of type 1 diabetes with hyperglycemia, ketosis, metabolic acidosis, dehydration, and altered consciousness

subject: Encephalitis (6th year)
description: Encephalitis: inflammation of brain parenchyma, usually viral (e.g., HSV), presenting with fever, altered consciousness, seizures, focal neurological deficits, CSF changes

subject: Endemic disease
description: Endemic disease: brucellosis, leishmaniasis, malaria, regional infections, endemic pediatric disease

subject: Epilepsy
description: Epilepsy: seizure disorder, recurrent seizures, EEG, antiepileptic drugs, focal seizure, generalized seizure

subject: Exanthematous
description: Exanthematous: measles, rubella, roseola, varicella, scarlet fever, rash with fever

subject: Febrile seizures & Status epilepticus
description: Febrile seizures & Status epilepticus: fever seizure, prolonged seizure, convulsion, benzodiazepines, emergency seizure management, status epilepticus

subject: Fluid therapy in children
description: Fluid therapy in children: maintenance fluids, dehydration, rehydration, electrolyte correction, pediatric IV fluids

subject: FTT obesity
description: FTT obesity: failure to thrive, obesity, malnutrition, BMI percentile, weight disorders

subject: functional abdominal pain (6th year)
description: Functional abdominal pain: recurrent abdominal pain without organic cause, related to gut-brain interaction, common in school-aged children

subject: GERD
description: GERD: gastroesophageal reflux, regurgitation, reflux disease, vomiting infant, esophagitis

subject: GI bleeding
description: GI bleeding: hematemesis, melena, lower GI bleeding, upper GI bleeding, anemia, endoscopy

subject: Growth in children & FTT
description: Growth in children & FTT: growth chart, developmental growth, failure to thrive, anthropometry, nutrition assessment

subject: Headache
description: Headache: migraine, tension headache, raised ICP, secondary headache, pediatric headache

subject: Hematological malignancies
description: Hematological malignancies: leukemia, lymphoma, bone marrow failure, blast cells, childhood blood cancer

subject: Hematuria & nephritis
description: Hematuria & nephritis: gross hematuria, microscopic hematuria, glomerulonephritis, post-streptococcal nephritis

subject: hemoglobinopathies (6th year)
description: Hemoglobinopathies: inherited disorders of hemoglobin (e.g., sickle cell disease, thalassemia), causing anemia, hemolysis, and related complications

subject: Hepatitis
description: Hepatitis: viral hepatitis, jaundice, liver enzymes, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, hepatomegaly

subject: HSP
description: HSP: Henoch-Schönlein purpura, IgA vasculitis, purpura, abdominal pain, nephritis, arthralgia

subject: HTN in children (6th year)
description: Hypertension in children: elevated blood pressure based on age/height percentiles, may be primary or secondary (renal/endocrine causes)

subject: HUS (6th year)
description: HUS: hemolytic uremic syndrome, AKI, thrombocytopenia, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, diarrhea-associated HUS

subject: Immunization
description: Immunization: vaccination schedule, childhood vaccines, EPI, contraindications, live vaccines, vaccine-preventable diseases

subject: Infant feeding
description: Infant feeding: breastfeeding, formula feeding, complementary feeding, weeding, nutrition in infancy

subject: Infant of diabetic mother
description: Infant of diabetic mother: IDM, macrosomia, neonatal hypoglycemia, polycythemia, congenital anomalies

subject: Introduction to allergic disorder
description: Introduction to allergic disorder: food allergy, atopy, eczema, allergic rhinitis, hypersensitivity, IgE mediated allergy

subject: JIA (Juvenile idiopathic arthritis)
description: JIA (Juvenile idiopathic arthritis): juvenile arthritis, chronic joint swelling, autoimmune arthritis, uveitis, pediatric rheumatology

subject: Kawasaki disease
description: Kawasaki disease: vasculitis, strawberry tongue, coronary artery aneurysm, prolonged fever, IVIG

subject: Meningitis
description: Meningitis: bacterial meningitis, neck stiffness, CSF analysis, meningeal signs, fever, altered consciousness

subject: metabolic acidosis and RTA (6th year)
description: Metabolic acidosis & RTA: acid-base disorders due to impaired renal acid handling, presenting with low bicarbonate, growth failure, and electrolyte imbalance

subject: Muscular dystrophy
description: Muscular dystrophy: Duchenne muscular dystrophy, proximal weakness, Gowers sign, dystrophin, calf pseudohypertrophy

subject: neonatal asphyxia & HIE (6th year)
description: Neonatal asphyxia & HIE: perinatal hypoxia leading to hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy, presenting with poor tone, seizures, altered consciousness, multi-organ injury

subject: Neonatal history
description: Neonatal history: birth history, antenatal history, natal history, postnatal history, gestational age, APGAR

subject: Neonatal Jaundice
description: Neonatal jaundice: physiological jaundice, pathological jaundice, unconjugated bilirubin, kernicterus, phototherapy

subject: neonatal resuscitation (6th year)
description: Neonatal resuscitation: immediate management of newborns with respiratory or circulatory compromise, including airway support, ventilation, chest compressions, and medications

subject: Neonatal seizures
description: Neonatal seizures: jitteriness, neonatal convulsions, hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy, metabolic causes, EEG

subject: Nephrotic syndrome & proteinuria
description: Nephrotic syndrome & proteinuria: edema, heavy proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, minimal change disease, nephrotic child

subject: neural tube defect (6th year)
description: Neural tube defect: congenital CNS malformations (spina bifida, anencephaly), due to failure of neural tube closure, associated with folate deficiency, neurological deficits

subject: Pediatric DM
description: Pediatric DM: type 1 diabetes, hyperglycemia, insulin, polyuria, polydipsia, diabetes in children

subject: Pediatric malignancy
description: Pediatric malignancy: solid tumors, leukemia, neuroblastoma, Wilms tumor, childhood cancer

subject: Pneumonia
description: Pneumonia: community acquired pneumonia, fever, cough, consolidation, bacterial pneumonia

subject: poisoning and insect bites (6th year)
description: Poisoning and insect bites: toxic exposure or envenomation in children, presenting with systemic or local symptoms, requiring supportive care and antidotes if indicated

subject: Puberty
description: Puberty: normal puberty, precocious puberty, delayed puberty, Tanner stage, endocrine maturation

subject: RDS
description: RDS: respiratory distress syndrome, surfactant deficiency, preterm infant, hyaline membrane disease

subject: respiratory failure (6th year)
description: Respiratory failure: inability to maintain adequate oxygenation or ventilation, presenting with hypoxia, hypercapnia, and respiratory distress

subject: Rheumatic fever
description: Rheumatic fever: post-streptococcal disease, Jones criteria, carditis, chorea, migratory arthritis

subject: Rickets
description: Rickets: vitamin D deficiency, bowed legs, hypocalcemia, metaphyseal changes, bone deformity

subject: short stature (6th year)
description: Short stature: height below expected percentile for age, causes include familial, endocrine (GH deficiency), chronic disease, or genetic syndromes

subject: Spinal muscle atrophy
description: Spinal muscle atrophy: SMA, hypotonia, motor neuron disease, tongue fasciculation, progressive weakness

subject: TB
description: TB: tuberculosis, chronic cough, contact history, Mantoux, pediatric TB

subject: Thyroid disorders
description: Thyroid disorders: hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, congenital hypothyroidism, goiter, thyroid function tests

subject: URTI
description: URTI: upper respiratory tract infection, common cold, pharyngitis, otitis, tonsillitis

subject: UTI
description: UTI: urinary tract infection, fever, dysuria, pyelonephritis, urine culture, vesicoureteral reflux

subject: Viral Neonatal Sepsis (6th year)
description: Viral neonatal sepsis: systemic viral infection in neonates (e.g., HSV, CMV), presenting with fever, lethargy, poor feeding, seizures, requiring antiviral therapy
